steps.title: Identify the shape
steps.content: The points rise faster as $x$ increases, so the model should be an increasing curve, not a straight line. That eliminates choices A and B.

steps.title: Compare choices C and D
steps.content: The curve that sits above all $8$ points is not centered on the data. The centered increasing curve has data points split around it: some above, some below, and some touching.

steps.title: Graph-check the centered curve
steps.content: A reasonable model should follow the trend and have points on both sides of the curve. The centered increasing curve with two points touching and points on both sides is correct.
